summary: Solid implementation with good PowerShell patterns (CmdletBinding, parameter validation, error handling, guard clause for dot-sourcing). Workflow uses pinned actions and least-privilege permissions. Main issues: (1) Pester install step (lines 13-17 in workflow) uses redundant conditional—should unconditionally install or remove the check; (2) Run-ActTests.ps1 hardcodes 7 expected values (16 tests, 8 passed, 6 failed, 2 skipped, 6.80s, 2 flaky, specific test names)—high coupling to fixture content makes adding test cases require manual editing; (3) Only 1 test case defined when task permits 3 act runs; (4) Relies on .actrc and ./fixtures/ existing but not provided in evaluation; (5) Markdown generation (lines 180-210) is dense and could benefit from more inline comments. Aggregator script is well-modularized with clear parsing dispatch; flaky test detection logic is sound. Workflow would fail without TestResultsAggregator.Tests.ps1 fixture files.
